subject: English
chapter: Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream
source_pdf: Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream.pdf

key_points: Verghese Kurien was an engineer who helped develop India’s dairy industry
He worked with dairy farmers in Anand and helped them form cooperative societies
Kurien’s efforts led to the success of the White Revolution in India
The cooperative system helped farmers sell milk directly and earn fair prices
His leadership contributed to the growth of dairy brands like AMUL
examples: Farmers joined cooperative societies to sell milk without middlemen
AMUL became a successful dairy brand managed by farmers
Operation Flood increased milk production across India
Milk collection centers helped farmers sell milk easily
Kurien’s work improved the income and living conditions of rural farmers
formulas_or_processes: Problem → Dairy farmers received low prices because of middlemen
Solution → Formation of dairy cooperatives led by farmers
Development → Programs like Operation Flood improved milk production
Result → Increased milk supply and better income for farmers
exam_tips: Remember that Verghese Kurien is known as the Father of the White Revolution
Understand how cooperative societies helped improve the dairy industry
Know the importance of AMUL and Operation Flood
Focus on the message that teamwork and innovation can improve society
